checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's text flow accurately. There are no signs of hallucinated paragraphs or sentences. The AI-generated metadata title and formatting (like markdown for emphasis) are within acceptable guidelines.
The output contains no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs. All contact information that is present correctly mirrors the source.

All ten pages of the source PDF are represented in the output. Gaps in the reading_order sequence (e.g., elements 13, 24, 32) correspond to page headers and footers that were logically filtered during the conversion process to preserve semantic flow, which is acceptable. However, specific functional data elements (URLs) were dropped within sections, which will be addressed in check C6.
The source document contains no tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there are omissions of specific data points (URLs), none of the omissions reach the threshold of 'significant' defined as more than approximately 25% of a section's text content. Most paragraphs are fully preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are two instances where specific URLs present in the source text are completely missing from the output, likely due to identification as separate elements that were then dropped. Specifically, 'www.ncstreams.net' is missing from Section 2, and 'www.nconemap.net' is missing from Section 7. These omissions represent a loss of functional information provided in the source.

available on the project website at www.ncstreams.net . The Upper French Broad

checks.b_evidence_location: Priority #2—Stream Mapping Project Status Report (Page 2, reading_order 15/17) and Orthoimagery Planning (Page 7, reading_order 60/62)

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any duplicated text blocks from the source. Each major paragraph and section (e.g., PRESENT members list, Priority #1-3, Committee reports, Action items) appears only once in the structured output, maintaining a 1:1 relationship with the source document's content segments.

The heading hierarchy is significantly corrupted in the latter half of the document. Under the 'Committee Reports' section (H2, element 54), the subsections are handled inconsistently. Earlier sub-committees (SMAC, SGUC, LGC) are rendered as paragraphs with bold/italic formatting. Later sub-committees (FIC, GIS TAC, M&O) are promoted to Level 2 headings (elements 74, 75, 76), making them siblings rather than children of 'Committee Reports'. Furthermore, these promoted headings (H2) incorrectly include the entire descriptive paragraph of body text within the heading text itself, rather than just the committee name.
checks.b_evidence_source: Federal Interagency Committee (FIC). Mary Combs reported on the NAIP photography (leaf-on) options for 2008 and the fact that North Carolina will not be flown in 2007. The FIC will continue to discuss land use planning around military installations in North Carolina as directed by the GICC.
checks.b_evidence_output: "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Federal Interagency Committee (FIC). Mary Combs reported on the NAIP photography (leaf-on) options for 2008 and the fact that North Carolina will not be flown in 2007. The FIC will continue to discuss land use planning around military installations in North Carolina as directed by the GICC."

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; it consists entirely of headers, paragraphs, and lists. Therefore, there is no table structure to be corrupted.

The overall flow of the document is preserved, following the logical sequence from the title and presence list through the proceedings and committee reports. Gaps in the reading order indices (e.g., 13, 24, 32) appear to correspond with page breaks or specific missing elements like URLs, but the remaining text blocks follow the source sequence accurately.

The numbered lists used for recommendations (e.g., (1) through (9) on pages 4-5) are converted to 'unordered' list types in the JSON (elements 40, 42). While the explicit numbers are preserved within the text, the semantic list type is incorrect. Additionally, Action items (Action 1-5) are handled inconsistently: some are converted into single-item unordered lists (elements 49, 51), while others are left as paragraphs (elements 65, 73, 82).
checks.b_evidence_source: (1) Avoid formal agreements, because this holds people back from sharing... (2) Web access to data is the basis of NC OneMap...
checks.b_evidence_output: "type": "list", "list_type": "unordered", "items": [ { "content": [ { "text": "(1) Avoid formal agreements..." } ] }, { "content": [ { "text": "(2) Web access to data..." } ] } ]

checks.a_analysis: Two key URLs present in the source text are missing from the output JSON. The link 'www.ncstreams.net' on page 2 and 'www.nconemap.net' on page 7 are omitted, leaving gaps in the paragraph text where the URLs originally appeared.
checks.b_evidence_source: All datasets, except for the Upper French Broad subbasin are now available on the project website at www.ncstreams.net .
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Joe Sewash said the Phase 1 of the North Carolina Stream Mapping Project in the western part of the state (19 counties) is complete. All datasets, except for the Upper French Broad subbasin are now available on the project website at "

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The document follows a standard single-column layout for meeting minutes, preserving the order of sections such as 'PRESENT', 'PROCEEDINGS', and the various 'Priority' status reports. The visual hierarchy of headings and the flow of paragraphs and lists accurately reflect the source's intent. Responsive design elements like the Table of Contents are added according to specifications and do not interfere with the logical layout.

The source PDF document consists entirely of text and lists; there are no data tables present within the pages provided. Consequently, no tables were rendered in the HTML, and no visual corruption occurred.
There are no meaningful images, logos, or charts in the source PDF. The document is strictly text-based. Therefore, no images were expected in the HTML output.

A thorough visual comparison confirms that no body content (paragraphs, list items) has been duplicated in the HTML output. While section headings appear in the Table of Contents as well as the body, this is an expected accessibility feature. The narrative content of the minutes flows linearly without repetition.
